title: Europe
subtitle: Post-Roman — illuminated manuscripts to post-impressionism.
intro: A thousand years of churches, altarpieces, and — eventually — secular portraits, landscapes, and the individual human strangeness at the turn of the 20th century.

works.title: Book of Kells
works.desc: A Latin gospel book from monks on Iona and Kells. The ornament is so intricate that Gerald of Wales thought it "the work of an angel, not of a man."

works.title: Chartres Cathedral
works.desc: Rebuilt in 26 years after a fire. Its stained glass — especially the "Chartres blue" — has never been matched; the recipe was lost.

works.title: The Arnolfini Portrait
works.desc: A merchant and his wife in a Bruges interior. The convex mirror on the back wall reflects two more figures — one of them possibly the painter.

works.title: The School of Athens
works.desc: Plato and Aristotle at the center, surrounded by the Greek philosophical tradition. Raphael slipped portraits of himself and Michelangelo into the crowd.

works.title: David
works.desc: Seventeen feet of marble carved from a block others had given up on. David faces Goliath before the stone flies — tense, not triumphant.

works.title: Sistine Chapel Ceiling
works.desc: Four years, mostly alone, on scaffolding. Michelangelo considered himself a sculptor, not a painter, and complained about the job the whole way through.

works.title: The Night Watch
works.desc: A militia portrait transformed into a theatrical scene — figures stepping forward, a drumbeat, a girl glowing in the crowd. Not actually set at night.

works.title: Las Meninas
works.desc: The Infanta and her attendants — but who is the painting for? The king and queen appear only in the mirror. Foucault wrote an essay on that mirror.

works.title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
works.desc: Not a portrait but a "tronie" — a study of an imagined figure. The pearl itself is two quick strokes of white paint. We don't know who she was.

works.title: The Starry Night
works.desc: Painted from the window of his asylum room at Saint-Rémy, from memory. The village below is imagined; the swirling sky he said he could actually see.

works.title: The Scream
works.desc: Munch wrote in his diary: "I sensed an infinite scream passing through nature." The sky's red may be from the Krakatoa eruption ten years earlier.
